title: British Prime Minister Starmer announces his resignation
summary: The Labour leader leaves office after two years in power due to falling popularity and internal divisions.

title: Sentenced to 27 years for the murder of a student in Asahikawa
summary: The Asahikawa court sentenced the defendant for the homicide of a minor, while a man burst into the courtroom.

title: Wave of crimes in Japan: gold scams, homicides and illegal trafficking
summary: Authorities report multiple criminal incidents, from fraud to murders, keeping the population on alert.

title: Japanese government pushes reform of the Imperial Household Law
summary: While the emperors travel to Belgium, the Executive presents a draft amendment to ensure imperial succession.

title: Japan accelerates development of artificial intelligence and technological innovation
summary: Japanese companies invest in AI, data centers and new technologies while the government promotes digital transformation.

title: Countries create communication network to ensure security in the Strait of Hormuz
summary: The US and Iran advance in talks to establish an alert system to prevent conflicts in the key maritime route.

title: Nikkei reaches new historical record exceeding 72,000 points
summary: The Japanese stock index continues its upward streak driven by the semiconductor sector and artificial intelligence.

title: Japanese Prime Minister assures that the reduction of consumption tax on food will be temporary
summary: The VAT reduction on basic products will be applied for two years and will be reversed without affecting public finances.

title: Controversy over the leak of the 'Red Princess' mandarin variety in China
summary: Ehime Prefecture requests government support regarding the possible illegal export of protected plants.

title: The 2026 World Cup continues with great performances and surprises
summary: Teams like Spain, Belgium and Brazil play key matches that define their positions in the tournament.

title: Japanese parliament debates taxes and defamatory videos
summary: In the budget committee, hot topics are discussed, while the opposition demands explanations from the Prime Minister.

title: Japan renews its railway network with the withdrawal of iconic trains
summary: The Shinkansen Series 500 says goodbye, while new overnight services are inaugurated and damaged sections are repaired.

title: Japanese sports celebrate achievements in golf, baseball and other disciplines
summary: From Yamashita's victory in golf to high school baseball championships, Japanese sports are experiencing outstanding moments.

title: Literary and artistic novelties enrich the Japanese cultural offer
summary: Exhibitions, books and intergenerational collaborations stand out in the country's cultural agenda.

title: Economic indicators and business movements mark Japan's financial landscape
summary: The market shows signs of growth with stock market records, wage increases and expansion of investments in various sectors.

title: Conservative candidate 'Tigre' wins the presidency of Colombia
summary: The former guerrilla and opera singer, supported by Trump, prevails in the second round of elections with a promise of a firm hand against drug trafficking.

title: Japan defeats Tunisia in the World Cup with brilliant team play
summary: The Japanese national team achieves a resounding victory with goals from Junya Ito and a solid defense that neutralized the opponent.

title: Cape Verde makes history by scoring its first goal in a World Cup
summary: The national team of the small African country gets a draw against Uruguay and celebrates its goal-scoring debut in the tournament.

title: 'King of drugs in Tohoku' and other members of the drug trafficking network arrested
summary: Miyagi Prefectural Police arrested several people linked to drug trafficking in the region.

title: Japanese yen depreciates to 161 yen per dollar on expectations of US rate hike
summary: The Japanese currency continues its downward trend as investors bet on a US monetary tightening.
